title: T-123 — Seed technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1 rule definition
prompt:
# T-123 — Seed technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1 rule definition

## Goal
Add the missing concrete rule seed for `technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1` so Settings/Auth policy preflights and Rule Variant UI can reference an actual deployed gate record.

This is a Settings rules seed/backlog task only. It does not implement NPD or Technical approval workflows.

## Files in scope
- rules/technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1.json [create]
- rules/_schemas/gate.schema.json [create/modify if missing]
- scripts/rules-deploy.test.ts [modify]
- apps/web/lib/settings/rules/technical-product-spec-approval-gate.test.ts [create if needed]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given T-026 rules deploy runs, then `rule_definitions` contains `rule_code='technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1'`, `rule_type='gate'`, `status='active'`, `priority='P1'`, and PRD refs `02-SETTINGS §3/§5.1`, `01-NPD post-release edit`, `03-TECHNICAL product-spec approval`.
- Given the seeded rule is loaded, then its condition/action metadata states: authorized NPD post-release edits must create a new BOM/product-spec version; factory use requires Technical approval; approval uses org_authorization_policies policy `technical_product_spec_approval`; self-approval is blocked when segregation-of-duties is required.
- Given T-122 validates V-SET-44, then it can resolve `approval_gate_rule_code='technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1'` to an active gate rule; missing/inactive/wrong type returns a typed blocker.
- Given SET-062 Rule Variant Selector renders, then the gate appears read-only and linked to Authorization Policies; variant selection cannot disable the gate or bypass `requires_new_version`.
- Seed is idempotent: re-running deploy with unchanged JSON is a no-op and does not bump version.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: deploy fixture/test fails because the rule seed is missing.
- GREEN: add rule JSON/schema metadata and idempotent deploy coverage.
- Run rules deploy tests and any targeted Settings rule tests.

## Risk red lines
- Do not author a UI rule editor; this is a seed/deploy task.
- Do not implement 01-NPD or 03-TECHNICAL workflows.
- Do not allow org variants to turn off the factory-use Technical approval invariant when `technical.product_spec_approval.required=true`.
- Do not seed the rule as P2/stub; it is P1 active per PRD table rule 32.

pipeline_inputs.description: Seed active P1 technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1 rule definition used by authorization-policy preflights and read-only rule views.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Create an idempotent rules deploy seed for technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1. It is the concrete rule_definitions row referenced by org_authorization_policies.approval_gate_rule_code and V-SET-44. The rule states that authorized NPD post-release edits create new BOM/product-spec versions and require Technical approval before factory use.
Preserved acceptance requirements: SET-062 can render the gate read-only and cannot bypass factory-use approval invariants.; Deploy is idempotent and unchanged JSON does not bump version.
acceptance_criteria: Active P1 gate rule technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1 is seeded into rule_definitions by T-026 deploy.
Rule metadata encodes new-version, Technical approval, org_authorization_policies, and segregation-of-duties invariants.
T-122 V-SET-44 preflight can resolve the active gate rule and returns typed blockers for missing/inactive/wrong type.
Additional preserved requirements in details are implemented and covered by targeted tests.
test_strategy: RED: deploy fixture/test fails because rule seed is missing.
GREEN: add rule JSON/schema metadata and deploy/idempotency coverage.
Run rules deploy tests and targeted Settings rule tests.
risk_red_lines: Do not author a UI rule editor.
Do not implement NPD or Technical workflows.
Do not allow variants/imports to disable the gate when technical.product_spec_approval.required=true.
Do not seed this as P2/stub.
